what_it_does: Registers a zero-argument head_builder thunk for the claude-code platform with the Mirror-Registry engine by importing the engine registrar and the runtime SessionSupervisor, wrapping a minimal call to SessionSupervisor._build_spawn_cmd, and invokes that registration at module import so a bare import wires the thunk.
observations: Contains a single function register_head_builders() and a module-level call to it.
Uses a try/except around engine and supervisor imports so wiring becomes a no-op when those modules are unavailable.
Registers only the claude-code platform head_builder via a lambda calling SessionSupervisor._build_spawn_cmd with claude_bin='claude', resume=None, fork=False.
The docstring states the file exists to keep platform rows pure data by moving thunk registration out of the row files.
The underscore-prefixed filename is intended to be skipped by platform file discovery and imported explicitly by runtime/tests.
Claims idempotency: re-registering the same thunk is a harmless dict overwrite.
standouts: Extensive docstring rationale for row purity and cycle discipline.
Import-time side effect (register_head_builders() called at module load).
Conditional registration via try/except rather than a hard dependency.
conventions: Module-level side effect after function definition.
Private-by-convention underscore prefix for a non-row wiring module.
concerns: Import-time side effect can make testing and import ordering surprising.
Broad except Exception swallows all import errors silently.
Hardcodes platform id 'claude-code' and claude_bin='claude' in wiring rather than deriving from row data.
Relies on a private method SessionSupervisor._build_spawn_cmd from another module.
notes: Wiring shim that trades a pure-data platform row for an import-time thunk registration side effect.
questions: What other platforms will add register_head_builder calls here?
How does the engine use or verify the registered thunk against declared transport_invariants?
What happens if the lambda signature or supervisor defaults change?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file registers a head_builder thunk outside the platform row and documents the purity motivation.
apparent_themes: Row purity / separation of data from behavior
Defensive wiring / graceful degradation when dependencies absent
Cycle discipline and explicit import contracts
intention_signals: Move the only behavioral dependency of platform rows (the head_builder thunk) into a dedicated, discoverable wiring module so the row files remain pure Pydantic-validatable dictionaries.
novelty: A wiring module whose explicit purpose is to register a thunk at import time, deliberately skipped by file discovery and designed to be a no-op in contexts that only need contract validation.
contribution: Provides an import-time registration hook that connects a platform's runtime spawn-command derivation to the mirror-registry engine without polluting the pure-data platform row, plus extensive rationale for cycle discipline and row purity.
summary_for_embedding: company/platforms/_wiring.py is an underscore-prefixed bootstrap module that registers the claude-code platform's head_builder thunk with the mirror-registry engine at import time, using a guarded import of the engine registrar and runtime SessionSupervisor.
intention_for_embedding: Keep platform row files pure data by moving live transport-invariant head derivation into a separate wiring module invoked explicitly by runtime and test contexts.

symbols.does: Registers platform head_builder thunks with the mirror-registry engine.
symbols.description: Imports the engine registrar and runtime SessionSupervisor inside a guarded try/except, then registers a zero-argument lambda that returns the unconditional spawn head for claude-code by calling SessionSupervisor._build_spawn_cmd with minimal arguments; called at module import to wire thunks on import.
